ogTitle: Greigs Flat vs Grenfell: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Greigs Flat and Grenfell.
heading: Greigs Flat vs Grenfell — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Greigs Flat, New South Wales and Grenfell, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Grenfell has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Greigs Flat (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Greigs Flat or Grenfell safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Greigs Flat has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Grenfell scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Greigs Flat scores 51/100 for bushfire risk. Grenfell scores 37/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Greigs Flat or Grenfell?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Grenfell has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.08% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
